req.title: Break-glass access (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall invoke break-glass access for an emergent patient; break-glass use shall be audited and trigger a post-event review. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that (1) a clinician can invoke break-glass access for an emergent patient and receive access, (2) every invocation emits an AuditEvent, and (3) a post-event review task is created. Test design covers: EP – valid clinician role triggers access + audit; NEG – non-clinician role is denied and audit still emitted; BVA – simultaneous duplicate break-glass requests (boundary on idempotency/deduplication); STC – state transition from normal-access → break-glass-active → post-review-created; DT – decision table covering role × emergency-flag combinations. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own patient and session resources.

tests.name: test_break_glass_grants_access_and_emits_audit_event
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role=clinician
A patient record exists with a known patient_id
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: At least one AuditEvent with action=break-glass and entity matching patient_id is present
trace: R-HLT-096: access granted on valid clinician break-glass invocation
R-HLT-096: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_break_glass_grants_access_and_emits_audit_event(
    clinician_session,   # <fixture>: returns authenticated requests.Session for role=clinician
    existing_patient,    # <fixture>: returns {"patient_id": "..."}  (created + torn down)
    fhir_base_url,       # <fixture>: returns base URL string
):
    patient_id = existing_patient["patient_id"]
    payload = {"patientId": patient_id, "reason": "emergency"}

    resp = clinician_session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("accessGranted") is True, "accessGranted must be True"

    audit_resp = clinician_session.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": patient_id, "action": "break-glass"},
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "At least one AuditEvent must exist"

    event = entries[-1]["resource"]
    assert event.get("outcome") == "0", "AuditEvent outcome must indicate success"
    assert event.get("action") == "break-glass"

tests.name: test_non_clinician_break_glass_is_denied_and_audit_emitted
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role=nurse (non-clinician)
A patient record exists with a known patient_id
actions: POST /BreakGlass with body {"patientId": "<patient_id>", "reason": "emergency"} using nurse session
Capture HTTP response status and body
GET /AuditEvent?entity=<patient_id>&action=break-glass&outcome=8 to check denied audit entry
expected: Response body contains accessGranted=false or equivalent denial message
An AuditEvent with outcome=8 (serious failure) is emitted for the denied attempt
trace: R-HLT-096: only role=clinician may invoke break-glass; others must be denied
R-HLT-096: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation including denied ones
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_clinician_break_glass_is_denied_and_audit_emitted(
    nurse_session,       # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session for role=nurse
    existing_patient,    # <fixture>: returns {"patient_id": "..."}
    fhir_base_url,       # <fixture>: base URL string
):
    patient_id = existing_patient["patient_id"]
    payload = {"patientId": patient_id, "reason": "emergency"}

    resp = nurse_session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    body = resp.json()
    access_granted = body.get("accessGranted", True)
    assert access_granted is False, "accessGranted must be False for unauthorized role"

    audit_resp = nurse_session.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": patient_id, "action": "break-glass", "outcome": "8"},
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "AuditEvent with outcome=8 must exist for denied break-glass"

tests.name: test_duplicate_break_glass_within_same_session_is_idempotent_and_single_review_task_created
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role=clinician
A patient record exists with a known patient_id
No prior break-glass session active for the patient
actions: Immediately POST /BreakGlass {"patientId": "<patient_id>", "reason": "emergency"} — second call (boundary: exactly 2 identical requests)
GET /Task?patient=<patient_id>&code=break-glass-review to count created review tasks
expected: Exactly one post-event review Task is created (idempotent deduplication at boundary)
Exactly two AuditEvents are emitted (one per operation call)
trace: R-HLT-096: break-glass use shall trigger a post-event review (not duplicated on retry)
R-HLT-096: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation (both calls audited)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_duplicate_break_glass_within_same_session_is_idempotent_and_single_review_task_created(
    clinician_session,   # <fixture>
    existing_patient,    # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,       # <fixture>
):
    patient_id = existing_patient["patient_id"]
    payload = {"patientId": patient_id, "reason": "emergency"}

    resp1 = clinician_session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload)
    assert resp1.status_code == 200, f"First call failed: {resp1.text}"
    assert resp1.json().get("accessGranted") is True

    resp2 = clinician_session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/BreakGlass", json=payload)
    assert resp2.status_code == 200, f"Second call failed: {resp2.text}"
    assert resp2.json().get("accessGranted") is True

    task_resp = clinician_session.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Task",
        params={"patient": patient_id, "code": "break-glass-review"},
    )
    assert task_resp.status_code == 200
    tasks = task_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(tasks) == 1, f"Expected exactly 1 review Task, found {len(tasks)}"

    audit_resp = clinician_session.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": patient_id, "action": "break-glass"},
    )
    events = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(events) >= 2, f"Expected at least 2 AuditEvents, found {len(events)}"

tests.name: test_break_glass_state_transitions_to_post_review_created
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role=clinician
A patient record exists with a known patient_id
No active break-glass session for the patient (state: NORMAL)
actions: Assert patient access state is NORMAL (no active break-glass Task)
Assert access is granted and AuditEvent emitted — state: BREAK_GLASS_ACTIVE
expected: Initial state: no active break-glass Task (NORMAL)
POST_REVIEW_CREATED: exactly one Task with status=requested and code=break-glass-review exists
trace: R-HLT-096: break-glass use shall trigger a post-event review
R-HLT-096: state transition NORMAL → BREAK_GLASS_ACTIVE → POST_REVIEW_CREATED
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_break_glass_state_transitions_to_post_review_created(
    clinician_session,   # <fixture>
    existing_patient,    # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,       # <fixture>
):
    patient_id = existing_patient["patient_id"]

    # Assert NORMAL state: no pre-existing review task
    pre_task = clinician_session.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Task",
        params={"patient": patient_id, "code": "break-glass-review"},
    )
    assert len(pre_task.json().get("entry", [])) == 0, "Pre-condition: no review task must exist"

    # Transition to BREAK_GLASS_ACTIVE
    resp = clinician_session.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/BreakGlass",
        json={"patientId": patient_id, "reason": "emergency"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json().get("accessGranted") is True

    # Verify AuditEvent (BREAK_GLASS_ACTIVE confirmed)
    audit = clinician_session.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": patient_id, "action": "break-glass", "outcome": "0"},
    )
    assert len(audit.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1, "AuditEvent with outcome=0 must exist"

    # Verify POST_REVIEW_CREATED state
    task_resp = clinician_session.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Task",
        params={"patient": patient_id, "code": "break-glass-review", "status": "requested"},
    )
    tasks = task_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(tasks) == 1, f"Expected 1 review Task in requested status, got {len(tasks)}"

tests.name: test_break_glass_decision_table_missing_reason_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role=clinician
A patient record exists with a known patient_id
actions: POST /BreakGlass with body {"patientId": "<patient_id>"} — reason field omitted (decision: role=clinician, emergency=absent)
Capture HTTP response status and body
GET /AuditEvent?entity=<patient_id>&action=break-glass to verify audit even on bad input
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing 'reason'
An AuditEvent with outcome=8 (failure) is still emitted for the attempted operation
trace: R-HLT-096: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation including malformed requests
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_break_glass_decision_table_missing_reason_rejected(
    clinician_session,   # <fixture>
    existing_patient,    # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,       # <fixture>
):
    patient_id = existing_patient["patient_id"]

    # Omit 'reason' to exercise the DT cell: valid role, missing emergency flag
    resp = clinician_session.post(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/BreakGlass",
        json={"patientId": patient_id},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400 for missing reason, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "reason" in error_text, "Validation error must reference missing 'reason' field"

    # Audit must still be emitted for the failed attempt
    audit_resp = clinician_session.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": patient_id, "action": "break-glass", "outcome": "8"},
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "AuditEvent outcome=8 must be emitted even for malformed break-glass request"
